name: Chocolate-Stuffed Peanut Butter Scones
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Butter (sweet, chilled), cut into 1/2-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees F.
2. In a large bowl, stir together the flour, brown sugar, baking powder, and salt.
3. Cut the butter into 1/2-inch cubes and distribute them over the flour mixture.
4. Using a pastry blender or two knives in a scissors fashion, cut in the butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
5. In a small bowl, stir together the peanut butter, milk, eggs, and vanilla extract.
6. Add the peanut butter mixture to the flour mixture and knead until combined.
7. Knead in the chopped peanuts.
8. Pat the dough out into a 1/2-inch thickness on a cutting board.
9. Using a floured 2-1/2-inch to 3-inch diameter biscuit cutter, cut out rounds from the dough. Gather the scraps together and repeat until all the dough is used and there are 16 rounds.
10. Place 8 of the rounds on an ungreased baking sheet.
11. Top each round with a piece of the chocolate and one of the remaining circles of dough.
12. Press the edges gently to seal.
13. Bake for 17 to 19 minutes, or until lightly browned.
14. Remove the baking sheet from the oven to a wire rack and cool for 5 minutes.
15. Using a spatula, transfer the scones to the wire rack to cool.
Variation:
Make the dough as above, omitting bittersweet chocolate. Substitute 1/2 cup unsalted whole peanuts for the chopped peanuts and knead in 3/4 cup semisweet chocolate chips at the same time. Pat the dough into a 9-inch diameter circle on a baking sheet. With a serrated knife, cut into 8 wedges. Bake for 20 to 22 minutes, or until a cake tester or toothpick inserted into the center of a scone comes out clean. Cool as above and recut into wedges, if necessary.
